summary: Scattered item-investigation cleanup and matching support across fighter, item, stage, effect, and menu code. The PR replaced many raw numeric IDs with named enums/constants, added item hold-kind and attribute documentation, implemented/typed ft_8008A348, tightened GObj classifier checks, and fixed an mnstagesel.static.h include guard collision that had broken a forward declaration.
what_changed: Added Item_HoldKinds in src/melee/it/forward.h with comments mapping hold categories such as common items, food, monster items, Pokemon items, character-related items, Coin, and Unk4.
Expanded item struct/attribute comments in src/melee/it/types.h, including notes that Item.xD4C is ammo remaining and that ItemAttr.x0_hold_kind should likely correspond to Item_HoldKinds.

Replaced raw GObj class numbers with named constants in multiple systems, including HSD_GOBJ_CLASS_EFFECT, HSD_GOBJ_CLASS_FIGHTER, HSD_GOBJ_CLASS_STAGE, HSD_GOBJ_CLASS_ITEM, and HSD_GOBJ_CLASS_ITEMLINK.

Replaced raw item-kind and motion-state numbers in fighter item-use code with named constants such as It_Kind_L_Gun, It_Kind_F_Flower, It_Kind_S_Scope, ftCo_MS_LGunShoot, ftCo_MS_LGunShootAir, ftCo_MS_LGunShootEmpty, and ftCo_MS_LGunShootAirEmpty.

Implemented ft_8008A348 in src/melee/ft/ft_0892.c and updated its header signature to void ft_8008A348(Fighter_GObj* gobj, float). The implementation transitions fighters toward wait/neutral handling, handles air-to-ground cleanup, Peach parasol cleanup, DeadUpFall handling, and Link/Young Link special follow-up calls.
Changed ft_800898A8 return type from UNK_T to struct Struct2074*, improving type precision for Fighter.x2074 access.

Clarified item pickup and held-item behavior in ftpickupitem.c, including comments for finding an item in pickup range and noting that Fighter.x1978 is the held item path used before item_gobj fallback.
Changed comparisons against 0 to boolean-style false in item/fighter helper calls such as ftpickupitem_80094790, it_8026B2B4, it_8026B594, and ftAnim_IsFramesRemaining.
Added classifier checks before treating GObjs as items or fighters in helpers such as it_80272D1C, it_80272D40, ftcommon sword checks, and ftlib classifier handling.
Fixed mnstagesel.static.h include guard from GALE01_2599EC to __GALE01_2599EC because it had collided with mnstagesel.h and caused the static header to be skipped.
Performed formatting/include-order cleanup across ftanim.c, ground.c, mnlanguage.c, mnsound.c, mnstagesel.c, and related files, mostly to support clearer matching-oriented edits.
smart_moves: Converted magic numeric constants to named enums and HSD_GOBJ_CLASS_* constants instead of leaving behavior encoded as raw values; this makes later item and fighter investigation searchable and less error-prone.
Kept small scattered investigation findings mergeable; reviewer feedback explicitly said small scattered PRs are beneficial because merging them early reduces future conflicts.
Used classifier checks before dereferencing or interpreting generic HSD_GObj pointers, reducing ambiguity in item/fighter helper code.
Documented uncertain item semantics inline without overclaiming, for example comments like 'Combine with Item_UnkKinds?', 'Should be enum', and question-marked behavior notes.
Fixed a subtle header-guard collision rather than chasing the downstream mnstagesel.c type error directly; the root cause was the static header being skipped.
decomp_lessons: When a C file reports confusing prototype or return-type errors, inspect include guards: duplicate guards between public and static headers can silently skip one header and break forward declarations.
Replacing raw GObj class integers with HSD_GOBJ_CLASS_* names improves both readability and type-safety reasoning across systems that share HSD_GObj.
For item investigation, distinguish item kind, hold kind, item action type, and fighter motion state; this PR added names/comments around all four without assuming full semantic certainty.
Use named fighter motion-state enums in Fighter_ChangeMotionState calls whenever possible; this makes ground/air and empty/non-empty variants obvious in LGun code.
Comments on fields such as Fighter.x1978 and Item.xD4C are useful even before full renaming, especially when they capture observed behavior like held item fallback or ammo checks.
Small decomp cleanup PRs can be worth merging quickly if they reduce conflict risk and preserve investigation breadcrumbs.
naming_conventions: GObj class constants use HSD_GOBJ_CLASS_* names, e.g. HSD_GOBJ_CLASS_ITEM, HSD_GOBJ_CLASS_FIGHTER, HSD_GOBJ_CLASS_STAGE, HSD_GOBJ_CLASS_EFFECT, HSD_GOBJ_CLASS_ITEMLINK.
Item kind enum names use It_Kind_* names, e.g. It_Kind_L_Gun, It_Kind_F_Flower, It_Kind_S_Scope, It_Kind_WStar, It_Kind_Peach_Parasol, It_Kind_L_Gun_Ray.
Common fighter motion-state names use ftCo_MS_* names, e.g. ftCo_MS_LightGet, ftCo_MS_HeavyGet, ftCo_MS_Wait, ftCo_MS_LGunShootAirEmpty.
Fighter part enum names use FtPart_* names, e.g. FtPart_TopN and FtPart_TransN, instead of raw indices where known.
New item hold-kind enum values are uppercase ITEM_HOLD_N style, with comments supplying provisional semantic categories.
Static/header guard fix used a distinct guard for mnstagesel.static.h: __GALE01_2599EC, avoiding reuse of mnstagesel.h's guard.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Implemented a previously stubbed function, ft_8008A348, with explicit early returns and an irregular switch for Link/Young Link follow-up behavior, preserving control-flow shape visible in the diff.
Used enum names while preserving the same numeric values, a low-risk tactic for readability that should not affect matching when types/constants compile to the same immediates.
Changed comparisons from == 0 to == false for boolean-returning helpers, clarifying intent without changing generated behavior in typical matching contexts.
Kept uncertain casts where needed, such as casting ftData_80085FD4 return to struct ftData_80085FD4_ret* before checking x8, rather than inventing broader type changes.
Refactored inline HSD_JObj traversal helpers in ftanim.c into multi-line static inline functions, likely for readability while preserving null-guarded next/parent/child semantics.
Used classifier guards around generic HSD_GObj inputs before item/fighter-specific logic, an important tactic when decomp code has weak pointer types.

review_feedback.author: ribbanya
review_feedback.feedback: The guard was the same as mnstagesel.h so the static header was being skipped, which broke the forward declaration.
review_feedback.resolution: Changed the guard from GALE01_2599EC to __GALE01_2599EC.

review_feedback.author: ribbanya
review_feedback.feedback: Small scattered PRs are not annoying; merging them is beneficial so conflicts do not occur later.

review_feedback.author: AaronB-Hub
review_feedback.feedback: Author noted being stumped by an mnstagesel.c error where the first call seemed to want an int return type; available evidence ties this to the skipped static header/forward declaration issue.

follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that rename or further type Item_HoldKinds and ItemAttr.x0_hold_kind.
Find later PRs that rename Fighter.x1978 or confirm held item semantics.
Find follow-up work on ft_8008A348, ftCo_MS_Wait transitions, or Link/Young Link cleanup behavior.
Search for remaining raw GObj_Create class constants that should become HSD_GOBJ_CLASS_*.
Search for remaining duplicate include guards between public headers and static headers.
